tasks.title: Initialize Project Repository and Development Environment
tasks.description: Set up the foundational project structure with Node.js 18+, Git repository, and development tooling
tasks.details: Create new Git repository, initialize with Node.js 18+ and pnpm workspace. Set up monorepo structure with packages/web, packages/ui, and packages/backend directories. Configure Cursor workspace with AI-powered development settings and recommended extensions (TypeScript, Tailwind CSS IntelliSense, MDX). Install essential development tools including Git hooks for commit linting.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify Node.js version compatibility, test pnpm workspace commands, confirm Cursor extensions are active, and validate Git repository initialization

subtasks.title: Initialize Git Repository and Set Up Core Structure
subtasks.description: Create a new directory for the monorepo, initialize a Git repository, and establish the foundational folder structure to support multiple projects and trauma-informed content handling.
subtasks.details: Run 'git init' in the root directory. Create subdirectories for apps, packages, and shared trauma-informed content modules. Add a README.md outlining the animal protection foundation's mission, emphasizing trauma-informed and donor trust-building principles.

subtasks.title: Set Up Node.js Environment and Configure pnpm Workspace
subtasks.description: Install Node.js and pnpm, then configure pnpm workspaces to manage multiple packages and apps within the monorepo.
subtasks.details: Install Node.js (v16+ recommended) and pnpm. Create a 'package.json' at the root with a 'workspaces' field listing all subprojects (e.g., 'apps/*', 'packages/*'). Ensure workspace structure supports modular emergency appeal functionality and content separation for trauma-informed handling.

subtasks.title: Configure Monorepo Structure for Foundation Needs
subtasks.description: Design and implement the monorepo folder and workspace structure to support trauma-informed content, donor trust-building, and emergency appeal modules.
subtasks.details: Create subfolders for each app (e.g., 'apps/website', 'apps/emergency-appeal') and shared packages (e.g., 'packages/trauma-content', 'packages/donor-trust'). Add placeholder files and documentation for each, specifying their roles in trauma-informed and donor-focused workflows.

subtasks.title: Set Up Cursor Workspace Configuration
subtasks.description: Create a Cursor workspace file and configure AI-powered development settings, recommended extensions, and settings to support collaborative, trauma-informed, and secure development.
subtasks.details: Create a .cursor/ directory structure with workspace configuration files. Set up .cursor/settings.json with AI model preferences, code completion settings, and foundation-specific configurations. Configure .cursor/workspace.json to include all relevant project folders and AI-assisted development settings. Leverage existing .cursor/rules/ directory for project-specific AI guidance. Document Cursor's AI capabilities for animal protection foundation development, including context-aware suggestions for donor management, compliance requirements, and sensitive data handling. Establish workspace recommendations for utilizing Cursor's AI features while maintaining security and donor trust standards.

subtasks.title: Implement and Configure Git Hooks for Quality and Compliance
subtasks.description: Set up Git hooks (using tools like Husky or simple scripts) to enforce trauma-informed content checks, donor trust guidelines, and emergency appeal workflow compliance on commit and push.
subtasks.details: Install Husky or similar tool. Configure pre-commit and pre-push hooks to run linting, check for sensitive content patterns, and validate emergency appeal modules. Document hook policies to ensure all contributions align with foundation values and compliance needs.

tasks.title: Configure Next.js 14 with App Router and TypeScript
tasks.description: Set up Next.js 15 framework with App Router, TypeScript configuration, and essential build optimizations using MCP-enhanced workflow with Context7 integration
tasks.details: Install Next.js 15.0+ with App Router enabled, configure TypeScript with strict mode, set up next.config.js with image optimization and MDX support. Configure tsconfig.json with path mapping for clean imports. Enable experimental features for React Server Components and static export optimization. Use Context7 MCP to verify latest 2025 best practices and avoid outdated patterns.
tasks.testStrategy: Create test page in app directory, verify TypeScript compilation against Context7 documentation, test hot reload functionality, confirm App Router navigation works with Next.js 15 patterns, and validate MCP workflow integration

subtasks.title: Research Next.js 15 Best Practices via Context7 MCP
subtasks.description: Use Context7 MCP to gather the latest Next.js 15 App Router patterns, configuration options, and 2025 best practices before beginning implementation.
subtasks.details: Query Context7 MCP for Next.js 15 specific documentation, App Router patterns, TypeScript configurations, and any breaking changes from Next.js 14. Document findings and create implementation plan based on latest 2025 standards.

subtasks.title: Install Next.js 15 with Required Dependencies
subtasks.description: Set up a new Next.js 15 project with the latest version and install all necessary dependencies, ensuring compatibility with TypeScript and 2025 best practices.
subtasks.details: Use `npx create-next-app@latest --ts` with Next.js 15 specifications. Verify exact version requirements through Context7 MCP documentation. Install additional dependencies following 2025 standards and ensure all packages are compatible with Next.js 15.

subtasks.title: Configure TypeScript for Trauma-Informed Content with Next.js 15 Standards
subtasks.description: Set up TypeScript with strict type checking and custom types/interfaces following Next.js 15 and 2025 TypeScript best practices for trauma-informed content structures.
subtasks.details: Configure `tsconfig.json` with Next.js 15 recommended settings verified through Context7 MCP. Define custom types/interfaces for content modules that handle sensitive animal protection stories, ensuring compatibility with Next.js 15 App Router patterns and latest TypeScript features.

subtasks.title: Customize next.config.js Using Next.js 15 Configuration Patterns
subtasks.description: Configure next.config.js following Next.js 15 best practices from Context7 MCP to support donor trust-building and emergency appeal features.
subtasks.details: Use exact configuration examples from Context7 MCP for Next.js 15. Configure security headers, image optimization, API routes, and redirects following 2025 standards. Avoid deprecated Next.js 14 patterns and implement new Next.js 15 features where applicable.

subtasks.title: Set Up Path Mapping with Next.js 15 Import Conventions
subtasks.description: Configure path mapping in tsconfig.json following Next.js 15 conventions verified through Context7 MCP for modular trauma-informed content organization.
subtasks.details: Implement path mapping using Next.js 15 recommended patterns from Context7 documentation. Add `paths` to `tsconfig.json` for key modules (`@content/*`, `@donors/*`, `@emergency/*`) ensuring compatibility with Next.js 15 App Router and 2025 import standards.

subtasks.title: Enable Next.js 15 Features and Validate Against Context7 Documentation
subtasks.description: Activate Next.js 15 specific features and experimental options that support foundation goals, validating each configuration against Context7 MCP documentation.
subtasks.details: Enable Next.js 15 features such as enhanced React Server Components, improved middleware, or new edge runtime capabilities. Cross-reference all configurations with Context7 MCP to ensure 2025 compliance and avoid deprecated patterns.

subtasks.title: Conduct Comprehensive Testing with Taskmaster-AI Progress Tracking
subtasks.description: Test the Next.js 15 setup comprehensively while tracking progress through taskmaster-ai subtask updates, ensuring all configurations meet 2025 standards.
subtasks.details: Run `next dev` and `next build` with Next.js 15. Test type safety, path mappings, and new features. Update progress through taskmaster-ai as each validation step completes. Simulate trauma-informed content, donor interactions, and emergency appeals using Next.js 15 patterns verified through Context7 MCP.

tasks.title: Integrate Tailwind CSS and Design System Foundation
tasks.description: Configure Tailwind CSS with custom theme for warm minimalism design system and responsive utilities
tasks.details: Install Tailwind CSS 3.4+, configure tailwind.config.js with custom color palette (deep sage green #2D5A27, warm coral #FA7C5A, warm grays), typography settings for optimal readability, and responsive breakpoints. Set up CSS custom properties for theme switching and accessibility preferences.
tasks.testStrategy: Test custom color classes, verify responsive utilities work across breakpoints, validate typography hierarchy, and confirm dark mode toggle functionality

subtasks.title: Install and Configure Tailwind CSS
subtasks.description: Set up Tailwind CSS in the project environment with necessary dependencies and initial configuration
subtasks.details: Install Tailwind CSS via npm, create the tailwind.config.js file, and set up the PostCSS configuration. Include the @tailwind directives in the main CSS file and configure the content paths to ensure proper purging in production.

subtasks.title: Customize Color Palette for Animal Protection Foundation
subtasks.description: Define a custom color palette that aligns with the foundation's branding while ensuring accessibility
subtasks.details: Configure theme.colors in tailwind.config.js with the foundation's brand colors. Include semantic color variables for trauma-informed design (calming colors for sensitive content). Ensure all colors meet WCAG AA contrast requirements for text legibility. Include specific color designations for emergency appeals and donation CTAs.

subtasks.title: Implement Typography System
subtasks.description: Set up typography defaults and custom styles for different content types
subtasks.details: Install and configure @tailwindcss/typography plugin. Customize font families, sizes, and weights in the theme configuration. Create specific typography styles for trauma-informed content, donor testimonials, and emergency appeals. Define heading hierarchies that maintain proper semantic structure while reflecting the foundation's visual identity.

subtasks.title: Configure Responsive Breakpoints
subtasks.description: Define custom breakpoints for responsive design that accommodate various device sizes and user contexts
subtasks.details: Customize the screens object in tailwind.config.js to define breakpoints that work well for the foundation's content. Create responsive variants for critical UI components like donation forms and emergency appeals. Implement a mobile-first approach with progressive enhancement for larger screens.

subtasks.title: Implement CSS Custom Properties for Dynamic Theming
subtasks.description: Set up CSS variables to enable dynamic theme switching and personalization
subtasks.details: Define CSS custom properties for color themes, spacing, and typography. Configure Tailwind to use these custom properties in the theme configuration. Create a system for theme switching that respects user preferences for reduced motion, contrast, and content sensitivity levels.

subtasks.title: Develop Theme Switching Functionality
subtasks.description: Create mechanisms for users to switch between light, dark, and high-contrast themes
subtasks.details: Implement JavaScript functionality to toggle between themes. Store user preferences in localStorage. Respect prefers-color-scheme media query for initial theme selection. Create UI controls for theme switching that are accessible via keyboard and screen readers. Include specific theme options for trauma-sensitive content viewing.

subtasks.title: Enhance Accessibility Features
subtasks.description: Implement additional accessibility enhancements beyond color contrast
subtasks.details: Configure focus styles that are visible in all themes. Create utility classes for screen-reader-only content. Implement reduced-motion variants for animations. Ensure all interactive elements have appropriate ARIA attributes. Create specific accessibility enhancements for donation forms and emergency appeals to build donor trust through inclusive design.

tasks.title: Install and Configure shadcn/ui Component Library
tasks.description: Set up shadcn/ui with Radix UI primitives for accessible, customizable components
tasks.details: Initialize shadcn/ui with npx shadcn-ui@latest init, configure components.json with custom theme tokens, install essential components (Button, Card, Input, Dialog, Sheet). Customize component styles to match warm minimalism design system. Set up component documentation and usage examples.

Implement responsive design considerations for all components to ensure mobile compatibility for emergency appeals.

Verify proper handling of sensitive content and appropriate display of trauma-informed elements.
